text.title: embedding-applications Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for embedding-applications as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Picking text-embedding-3-small because it ranks in the top 5 on MTEB without checking retrieval-specific score.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: text-embedding-3-small ranks 62.3% overall but only 51.7% on retrieval — a 12-15% gap compared to bge-m3's 66.8% retrieval score. In a 1000-document corpus, this can mean 100+ fewer correct results in top-10.

failure-mode.description: Benchmarking single texts one at a time because "that's the real use case".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Single-text latency is 5-20x higher than batched latency due to network/server initialization overhead. Production deployments always batch; single-text numbers are not comparable to real-world performance and will misdirect optimization efforts.

failure-mode.description: Inserting non-normalized vectors into pgvector and using the <=> (cosine) operator without checking index type.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unnormalized vectors give wrong rankings with cosine distance. The <=> operator requires a vector_cosine_ops index; creating the default L2 (vector_l2_ops) index silently produces incorrect results. Agents commonly introduce this bug because the error is silent — queries return results with no error message, but the ranking is wrong.

failure-mode.description: Changing the embedding dimension or distance metric after inserting millions of documents.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Most vector databases do not support schema changes on live collections. Changing dimension or metric requires dropping and re-creating the collection, re-embedding all documents, and re-inserting. Always set these parameters at creation time and validate them before upsert.

failure-mode.description: Running benchmarking and insertion in series without quality gates, assuming "newer models are always better".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Newer embedding models often degrade on niche vocabularies. Without benchmarking, you can quietly degrade retrieval quality by 10-20% after a model upgrade. Quality gates prevent silent regressions and force explicit measurement.

failure-mode.description: Embedding documents with input_type="search_query" because "it's all the same function".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cohere (and Voyage) specifically train dual spaces for documents vs. queries; using the wrong type breaks symmetry and reduces retrieval quality by 5-15%.

failure-mode.description: Selecting an embedding model solely based on MTEB leaderboard position #1-3 without checking retrieval-specific scores.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: MTEB aggregates 56 tasks. A model that ranks #1 overall might rank #15 on retrieval if it excels at classification or clustering instead. Domain vocabulary (legal, biomedical, code) is often undertrained in general-purpose embeddings. Custom benchmarking with 50-100 labeled pairs on your domain prevents the costly mistake.

failure-mode.description: Assuming embedding API changes are backward-compatible ("it's just embedding").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OpenAI changed text-embedding-3-* models silently multiple times; users relying on consistency had to re-embed million-document corpora unannounced. Always store model name, version, and embedding date in collection metadata and re-embed on model changes.

failure-mode.description: Tuning HNSW parameters (m, ef_construct) after inserting millions of documents.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: HNSW parameters are set at collection creation time; changing them requires re-indexing the entire collection (hours to days for large corpora). Always set these at creation time based on latency/recall SLAs.
